correctness.justification: All 11/11 acceptance tests pass. Implementation correctly handles backpressure via bounded queue, concurrent worker execution, draining on exit, error propagation, and all specified behaviors.

edge_cases.justification: All edge cases handled: empty pool (no submissions), max_queue=1 backpressure, single worker serial execution, submit-after-exit raises RuntimeError, zero/negative max_workers and max_queue raise ValueError with descriptive messages, exceptions drain remaining work before re-raising.

code_quality.justification: Excellent type hints throughout, clean separation of concerns (_Pool class, _worker function, bounded_pool context manager), idiomatic use of asynccontextmanager, sentinel pattern for shutdown, proper use of asyncio primitives. Minor: _record_error is a 'private' method called from module-level code, but acceptable within same module.

documentation.justification: Comprehensive docstrings with Args, Raises, and Examples sections on all public functions. Module-level docstring present. Internal functions (_worker, _record_error) have adequate docstrings. The bounded_pool docstring clearly explains backpressure semantics and drain behavior.

test_coverage.justification: Exceptional test coverage: happy path (zero tasks, all tasks complete, concurrent execution, serial with max_workers=1, backpressure, kwargs forwarding), error handling (exception re-raised, remaining tasks drain after exception, submit after exit), input validation (max_queue=0, max_workers=0, negative values), and a stress test with 100 tasks. Each test has a clear docstring explaining its intent.

robustness.justification: Strong robustness: validates max_workers and max_queue with clear ValueError messages, marks pool as closed on exit to prevent post-exit submissions, captures worker exceptions without crashing other workers, uses queue.join() to ensure complete draining before re-raising. The only minor gap is that only the first error is re-raised (others are silently dropped), which is a reasonable design choice but could be documented more explicitly as a limitation.
